stem:
Consider the following relations A, B and C:

A:
ID | Name   | Age
12 | Arun   | 60
15 | Shreya | 24
99 | Rohit  | 11

B:
ID | Name   | Age
15 | Shreya | 24
25 | Hari   | 40
98 | Rohit  | 11

C:
ID | Phone | Area
10 | 2200  | 01
99 | 2100  | 01

How many tuples does the result of the following relational algebra expression contain? Assume that the schema of A ∪ B is the same as that of A.

(A ∪ B) ⋈_{A.ID = C.ID} C
reason: Parser mistook the table data rows as answer options A/B/C and merged the real question text and numeric options into option C/D. Stem should include all table data and the actual question; options should be 7, 4, 5, 9.

stem:
Consider the following relational schema:
COURSES (cno, cname)
STUDENTS (rollno, sname, age, year)
REGISTERED_FOR (cno, rollno)

The underlined attributes indicate the primary keys for the relations. The 'year' attribute for the STUDENTS relation indicates the year in which the student is currently studying (First year, Second year etc.).

A. Write a relational algebra query to print the roll number of students who have registered for cno 322.
B. Write a SQL query to print the age and year of the youngest student in each year.

For part A, the relational algebra expression is:
reason: Parser treated the two sub-question descriptions (A. Write a relational algebra query... and B. Write a SQL query...) as answer options A and B, and merged the real options into option B/C/D. Stem should include all setup text including the sub-questions and the concluding line 'For part A, the relational algebra expression is:'; options should be the four relational algebra expressions.
